title: [Bug]: Rootless custom network ignores MTU setting in Quadlet (.network) and defaults to 65520
body:
### Issue Description

In Podman 5.7.0 (rootless), custom bridge networks created via Quadlet ignore the mtu option. Even when `Options=mtu=1500` is specified in a `.network` file and `Network=name:mtu=1500` is used in a .container file, the container's interface defaults to 65520.

This causes significant issues for UDP-based real-time applications (specifically FlightAware PiAware MLAT) that require a standard 1500 MTU for internet-bound timing packets. The MTU inside the container "flaps" between 1500 and 65520, causing connection instability.

Attempting to manually set the MTU inside the container via `Exec=` fails with "Operation not permitted" (unless `CAP_NET_ADMIN` is added), and even with that capability, it often interferes with the container's init system (`s6-overlay`).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
Create a Quadlet network file (`adsb.network`) with:

```
[Network]
NetworkName=adsb
Options=mtu=1500
```

Create a Quadlet container file linking to that network.

Start the service and run: `podman exec -it <container> ip link show eth0`

Observe that MTU is `65520` instead of `1500`.

Note that podman network inspect adsb may show the option is set, but the actual interface in the rootless namespace does not honor it.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received
The container interface stays at 65520. Application logs show:
`mlat-client: Route MTU changed to 65520`
This leads to packet fragmentation and loss for UDP traffic destined for the WAN.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
The container interface should honor the MTU specified in the Quadlet `.network` or `.container` file, especially when using the bridge driver in rootless mode.

### podman info output

comments.body:
Did you actually inspect the network and check that the mtu is applied? quadlet just calls the cli so it will not really have any different behaviour except that the networks are created exactly once and not removed. So if you edit the file later it will not recreate the network.


This works just fine as rootless:
```
$ podman network create --opt mtu=1234 testnet
testnet
$ podman run --rm --network testnet quay.io/libpod/testimage:20241011 ip link
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN mode DEFAULT group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
2: eth0@if4: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1234 qdisc noqueue state UP mode DEFAULT group default qlen 1000
    link/ether c2:52:47:e1:38:82 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff link-netnsid 0
```


> Network=name:mtu=1500

That is and was never a valid option for the individual container network config so this cannot work.
https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-create.1.html#network-mode-net

Mtu is a network wide option and the example
```
[Network]
NetworkName=adsb
Options=mtu=1500
```

works fine here. I guess you just did not remove the network.
